question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_PORT to '65535' via AutomaticEnv() cause the HTTP server to bind to a privileged or conflicting port, disrupting legitimate fee-related API traffic and allowing an attacker to intercept fee construction requests on the original port?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can write an application.yml to the CWD set hiero.mirror.rosetta.network to 'previewnet', causing the construction service to use the Hedera preview network's fee schedule (which may differ significantly from mainnet), resulting in transaction fees outside the design parameters intended for production?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can write an application.yml to the CWD set hiero.mirror.rosetta.network to 'other' and provide a custom nodes map, causing the construction service to use hiero.ClientForNetworkV2() with attacker-controlled nodes that return arbitrary fee schedules outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NETWORK to 'OTHER' (uppercase) via AutomaticEnv() bypass the strings.ToLower() normalization applied to the Network field, causing the construction service to fail to match the network name and fall back to an unintended network with a different fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NETWORK to a network name with leading/trailing whitespace via AutomaticEnv() cause the network string to not match any known network after strings.ToLower(), resulting in the SDK client failing to initialize correctly and using a default fee schedule outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_CACHE_ENTITY_MAXSIZE to '1' via AutomaticEnv() reduce the entity cache to a single entry, causing near-constant cache misses for fee-related entity lookups and forcing repeated database queries that may return inconsistent fee data under concurrent modification?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_PORT to '65535' via AutomaticEnv() cause the HTTP server to bind to a privileged or conflicting port, disrupting legitimate fee-related API traffic and allowing an attacker to intercept fee construction requests on the original port?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can write an application.yml to the CWD set hiero.mirror.rosetta.network to 'previewnet', causing the construction service to use the Hedera preview network's fee schedule (which may differ significantly from mainnet), resulting in transaction fees outside the design parameters intended for production?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can write an application.yml to the CWD set hiero.mirror.rosetta.network to 'other' and provide a custom nodes map, causing the construction service to use hiero.ClientForNetworkV2() with attacker-controlled nodes that return arbitrary fee schedules outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NETWORK to 'OTHER' (uppercase) via AutomaticEnv() bypass the strings.ToLower() normalization applied to the Network field, causing the construction service to fail to match the network name and fall back to an unintended network with a different fee schedule?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/config.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: LoadConfig()] Can an unprivileged external user who can set HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_NETWORK to a network name with leading/trailing whitespace via AutomaticEnv() cause the network string to not match any known network after strings.ToLower(), resulting in the SDK client failing to initialize correctly and using a default fee schedule outside design parameters?
